title: नारायणाष्टकम्
devanagariText:
॥ श्री नारायणाष्टकम् ॥

वसुदेवसुतं देवं कंसचाणूरमर्दनम्।
देवकीपरमानन्दं कृष्णं वन्दे जगद्गुरुम्॥ १॥

नारायणं सर्वभूतनिवासं नारायणम्।
क्षीरसागरवासिनं नारायणं भजे सदा॥ २॥

नारायण हरे नारायण हरे
नारायण हरे नारायण हरे।
हरिं नारायणं सत्यं ज्ञानमनन्तं पुरातनम्।
विश्वरूपं सहस्राक्षं नारायणमहं भजे॥ ३॥

यत्कारणं विश्वसृजामधीशं
नारायणं भक्तजनार्तिनाशम्।
धर्मैकरक्षं जगतां विधातारं
भक्त्या नरो मुच्यत संसृतेर्भयात्॥ ४॥

नमो नारायणायेति मन्त्रैः पापक्षयं नरः।
सम्प्राप्नोति परं स्थानं यत्रोच्चैर्नारदो वदन्॥ ५॥

नारायणाय शुद्धाय शाश्वताय ध्रुवाय च।
सर्वव्यापिनमव्यक्तमनादिनिधनं भजे॥ ६॥

नारायणः परं ब्रह्म तत्त्वं नारायणः परः।
नारायणः परो ज्योतिरात्मा नारायणः परः॥ ७॥

नारायणः परो ध्याता ध्यानं नारायणः परः।
यच्च किंचिज्जगत्सर्वं दृश्यते श्रूयतेऽपि वा।
अन्तर्बहिश्च तत्सर्वं व्याप्य नारायणः स्थितः॥ ८॥

॥ इति श्री नारायणाष्टकम् सम्पूर्णम् ॥
transliteration:
|| Shri Narayana Ashtakam ||

Vasudevasutam devam Kamsa-Chanura-mardanam.
Devaki-paramanandaam Krishnam vande Jagadgurum. || 1 ||

Narayanam sarva-bhuta-nivasam Narayanam.
Kshira-sagara-vasinam Narayanam bhaje sada. || 2 ||

Narayana Hare Narayana Hare
Narayana Hare Narayana Hare.
Harim Narayanam satyam jnanam-anantam puratnam.
Vishva-rupam sahasra-aksham Narayanam-aham bhaje. || 3 ||

Yat-karanam vishva-srijam-adhisham
Narayanam bhakta-jana-arti-nasham.
Dharma-eka-raksham jagatam vidhataram
Bhaktya naro muchyata samsriter-bhayat. || 4 ||

Namo Narayanaya-iti mantraih papa-kshayam narah.
Samprapnoti param sthanam yatra-uchchaih Narado vadan. || 5 ||

Narayanaya shuddhaya shashvataya dhruvaya cha.
Sarva-vyapinam-avyaktam-anadi-nidhanam bhaje. || 6 ||

Narayanah param Brahma tattvam Narayanah parah.
Narayanah paro jyotir-atma Narayanah parah. || 7 ||

Narayanah paro dhyata dhyanam Narayanah parah.
Yach-cha kimchij-jagat-sarvam drishyate shruyate-api va.
Antar-bahish-cha tat-sarvam vyapya Narayanah sthitah. || 8 ||

|| Iti Shri Narayana Ashtakam Sampurnam ||
hindiMeaning:
वसुदेव के पुत्र, कंस और चाणूर को मर्दन करने वाले, देवकी के परमानन्द, जगद्गुरु कृष्ण को मैं वन्दन करता हूँ॥ १॥

सर्वव्यापी, सब प्राणियों में निवास करने वाले, क्षीरसागर में विराजमान नारायण को मैं सदा भजता हूँ॥ २॥

नारायण हरे, नारायण हरे! सत्य, ज्ञान, अनन्त, पुरातन, विश्वरूप, सहस्रनेत्र हरि नारायण को मैं भजता हूँ॥ ३॥

जो विश्व के सृष्टिकर्ताओं के भी अधीश्वर हैं, भक्तों की आर्ति (पीड़ा) का नाश करने वाले, धर्म के एकमात्र रक्षक, जगत के विधाता — उन नारायण को भक्तिपूर्वक भजने से मनुष्य संसार के भय से मुक्त होता है॥ ४॥

'नमो नारायणाय' इस मन्त्र से मनुष्य पापक्षय प्राप्त करता है और परम स्थान को प्राप्त होता है — जहाँ नारद उच्च स्वर से यही कहते हैं॥ ५॥

नारायण परम ब्रह्म हैं, नारायण परम तत्त्व हैं। नारायण परम ज्योति हैं, नारायण परमात्मा हैं। नारायण ही परम ध्याता और ध्यान हैं। जो कुछ भी जगत में दृश्य या श्रव्य है — उन सबको अन्दर-बाहर से व्याप्त करके नारायण स्थित हैं॥ ७-८॥
benefits: Simple yet profound prayer invoking Lord Narayana's all-pervading presence
Traditionally recited for removal of all sins through the power of narayana's name
Invokes liberation from the fear of samsara (material existence)
Contains the essence of Narayana Upanishad in verse 7-8
Ideal for daily morning and evening recitation
Invokes the blessing of peace, protection, and spiritual elevation
description: Narayana Ashtakam (नारायणाष्टकम्) is an eight-verse devotional hymn dedicated to Lord Vishnu. Attributed to Traditional Vaishnavite Literature, this prayer holds a special place in Hindu devotional literature. Regular recitation is believed to bestow peace, protection, and spiritual elevation, simple yet profound prayer invoking Lord Narayana's all-pervading presence, and destroy all sins through the power of Narayana's name. Recitation is particularly auspicious on Thursday (Guruvar) and Sunday (Ravivar) and during Ekadashi and Vaikuntha Ekadashi. With 8 verses and a reading time of about 5 minutes, this prayer is ideal for both daily worship and special occasions. On this page, you can read the complete Narayana Ashtakam in Devanagari Sanskrit with English transliteration, Hindi meaning (arth), and free PDF download for offline recitation.
seoDescription: Narayana Ashtakam in Sanskrit with Hindi meaning. Eight verses glorifying Lord Narayana as the supreme all-pervading reality.
relatedSearches: Narayana Ashtakam Sanskrit lyrics with meaning PDF

Bhakti yoga via Narayana name chanting
Narayana eight verses for daily recitation
Vishnu nama smaranam path to moksha

seoFaqs.question: What is the Narayana Ashtakam and what is its theme?
seoFaqs.answer: The Narayana Ashtakam is an eight-verse Sanskrit hymn (ashtakam = eight-verse) opening with Vasudeva-sutam devam Kamsa-Chanura-mardanam — saluting Lord Krishna as the son of Vasudeva and slayer of Kamsa and Chanura. The hymn is preserved in the traditional Vaishnavite stotra literature and combines Krishna-bhakti with Narayana-tattva exposition, declaring Narayana as the supreme Brahman, the all-pervading reality, the consciousness that pervades both inside and outside everything visible and audible (verses 7-8). The closing verses borrow directly from the Narayana Upanishad. The hymn is widely recited as a daily Vishnu-stuti in South Indian Vaishnava traditions. (Sources: traditional Vaishnavite stotra anthologies; Vedadhara Vishnu texts.)

seoFaqs.question: Why does the Narayana Ashtakam open with a verse to Krishna?
seoFaqs.answer: The opening verse Vasudeva-sutam devam Kamsa-Chanura-mardanam — saluting the son of Vasudeva, slayer of Kamsa and Chanura, the supreme delight of Devaki, Krishna the world-teacher — establishes the Vaishnava doctrine that Krishna is identical with Narayana, not a mere avatara but the purna avatara (full descent) of the supreme being. This is the famous Krishnas tu Bhagavan svayam declaration of the Bhagavata Purana (1.3.28). By opening with Krishna and then praising the same being as Narayana in the subsequent verses, the hymn affirms the non-difference of the personal-incarnate (Krishna) and the cosmic-impersonal (Narayana) aspects of the supreme Vishnu-tattva. (Sources: Bhagavata Purana 1.3.28; Vaishnava theology.)

seoFaqs.question: What is the Ashtakshari Narayana mantra mentioned in verse 5?
seoFaqs.answer: Verse 5 declares Namo Narayanaya iti mantraih papa-kshayam narah — by the mantra Om Namo Narayanaya, a person attains the destruction of sins. This is the famous Ashtakshari (eight-syllable) mantra Om Namo Narayanaya — the most fundamental Vaishnava mantra, considered the Mula Mantra of the Pancharatra Agama and the principal initiation mantra in Sri Vaishnavism (Tirumantra). The verse adds yatra-uchchaih Narado vadan — wherever the celestial sage Narada loudly proclaims this mantra. Narada is the supreme devotee whose continuous chanting of Narayana's name through the three worlds is celebrated in the Bhagavata, Padma and Narada Puranas. (Sources: Pancharatra Agama; Sri Vaishnava Tirumantra tradition; Bhagavata Purana.)

seoFaqs.question: What is the meaning of the famous closing verse Narayanah param Brahma?
seoFaqs.answer: Verse 7 declares Narayanah param Brahma tattvam Narayanah parah, Narayanah paro jyotir-atma Narayanah parah — Narayana is the supreme Brahman, Narayana is the supreme Tattva, Narayana is the supreme Light, Narayana is the supreme Atman. Verse 8 adds Antar-bahish-cha tat-sarvam vyapya Narayanah sthitah — Narayana stands pervading all this from inside and outside. These two verses are taken almost verbatim from the Narayana Upanishad (also called Narayana Suktam) of the Krishna Yajurveda, and they constitute one of the most celebrated philosophical declarations in all of Vaishnava theology — affirming Narayana as the one absolute reality, the inner Self of all beings. (Sources: Narayana Upanishad; Mahanarayana Upanishad.)

seoFaqs.question: When and how is the Narayana Ashtakam traditionally recited?
seoFaqs.answer: The Narayana Ashtakam is recited daily by Vaishnava devotees, especially in the morning after sandhyavandanam and bath, facing east before a Vishnu or Krishna image. It is given special weight on Thursdays (Guruvar, sacred to Vishnu and Brihaspati) and Sundays, on every Ekadashi (the eleventh day of each lunar fortnight, sacred to Vishnu), and on the great festival days of Vaikuntha Ekadashi (Margashirsha Shukla Ekadashi), Ram Navami, Krishna Janmashtami, Vamana Jayanti and Hari Vasara. It is also chanted as an upachara (offering) during temple darshan and as a daily prayer for protection from samsara-bhaya (the fear of cyclic existence) as verse 4 specifies. (Sources: Vaishnava daily anushthana manuals.)

seoFaqs.question: What benefits does the Narayana Ashtakam promise?
seoFaqs.answer: The hymn explicitly promises three principal benefits across its verses. Verse 4 states bhaktya naro muchyata samsriter-bhayat — the devotee is freed from the fear of samsara (cyclic existence) by devotion to Narayana. Verse 5 promises papa-kshayam (destruction of sins) and attainment of the supreme abode (param sthanam) through chanting Om Namo Narayanaya. Verse 6 promises union with the eternal, unchanging, all-pervading Narayana through meditation. Traditional commentaries add peace of mind, protection from untimely death, removal of obstacles in spiritual practice, and ultimately moksha through the gradual ripening of bhakti yoga via continuous nama-smarana. (Sources: traditional Vaishnava commentaries; Narayana Upanishad.)
